title: Manganaroa martii
taxonomy: Manganaroa martii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It is a member of the genus Manganaroa, which is endemic to New Zealand. The plant is part of the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. The family Lamiaceae is a diverse group of plants with over 7,000 species worldwide. Manganaroa martii is a unique and specialized species within this family.

preparations: Manganaroa martii herbal preparations are primarily used by the indigenous people of New Zealand for medicinal purposes, including treating fever and infections.
